scheme.name: HearthCode
scheme.headline: Warm-neutral code color language
scheme.summary: A warm, low-glare color philosophy for code interfaces and reading surfaces.

ground.description: Primary environment ground for the canvas field across all variants.

carrier.description: Readable carrier environment used as the long-session ink anchor.

shell.description: Sheltering chrome shell for navigation, rails, and supportive UI housing.

ash.description: Quiet guidance neutrals for comments and soft supporting ink.

ember.description: Warm control-flow anchor with brick-red pressure.

mineral.description: Cool structural anchor used sparingly to balance the warm field.

leather.description: Secondary action warmth for methods and callable side-notes.

olive.description: Muted structural green for properties and member access.

moss.description: Calm literal band for strings and reading rhythm.

terracotta.description: Numeric warmth for constants and measured emphasis.

ochre.description: Dry structure hue for types and classification signals.

chalk.description: Readable carrier neutrals for variables and parameters.

cedar.description: Warm tag and markup accent that stays close to ember without collapsing into it.

ink.description: Readable carrier ink anchor for long sessions.

panel.description: Raised document substrate mixed up from canvas toward ink.

gutter.description: Quiet numeric gutter derived as a mid-band between canvas and ink.

sidebar.description: Sheltering shell anchor for navigation and chrome.

border.description: Structural edge derived from the same canvas-ink band, but kept quieter than gutter.

cursor.description: Primary live cursor and accent signal.

status.description: Persistent emphasis band used for status presence and release accents.

focusRing.description: Focusable edge signal used for ring and border emphasis.

lineEmphasis.description: Current-line substrate emphasis without becoming a spotlight.

listHover.description: Hover substrate for navigational lists and tabs.
